entry.title: Re: Secondary Index - Improving DSO insert/read performance
entry.content: Good Article .

entry.title: Re: Will deprecation of Learning Token Server change the...
entry.content: Hi @PhaniAZJ, that's correct. The base Learning URL "https://xxxxxx.plateau.com" will not change for the API calls.

entry.title: Re: Change WBS Element Status FIORI App is not showing WB...
entry.content: Yes, all required configurations are in place and the access is working when tested with SAP_ALL assignment. However, unfortunately, nothing is getting triggered or captured in the trace or system logs, which is making it difficult to identify the exact authorization check or failure point.

entry.title: Join our Espresso Session: Latest Innovations and Autonomous Asset Management for Utilities
entry.content:
Join us for a focused 30-minute Espresso Session for utilities and asset-intensive organizations on June 23 at 4pm. CET. We will show how SAP Intelligent Asset Management is evolving with AI and automation. Discover how integrated asset management, AI-powered decision support, and Joule agents help utilities improve reliability, optimize resources, and achieve operational excellence.

Key Topics:

  • Predictive Maintenance: Learn how AI can forecast potential issues, spot risks early, and help prioritize maintenance to extend the life of equipment and reduce costs.
  • Optimized Field Service: Discover how AI and Joule help assign the right teams, plan the best routes, and support technicians to get work done faster and with fewer mistakes.
  • Actionable Insights and agentic workflows: Explore how data from all operations can be used to make better decisions, continuously improve processes, and align teams for better outcomes.

Who Should Join?

This session is perfect for maintenance managers, field service leaders, operations teams, and anyone responsible for maintenance or service delivery who want to use AI and intelligent tools to improve efficiency, reduce downtime, and enhance performance.

Register here

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 07 - Authorization annotations
Re: Week 2 – Building AI Agents with CrewAI and Generative AI Hub
entry.content: Excellent walkthrough of building an AI agent with CrewAI and SAP Generative AI Hub. The real-world welfare case management example makes agent concepts easy to understand and apply. Thanks for sharing! 🚀

entry.title: Re: Week 4: AI Developer Challenge SAP-RPT-1 Model
entry.content: Excellent challenge! It's fascinating to see SAP-RPT-1 bringing foundation model capabilities to tabular enterprise data. The exercise of adjusting dataset fields to achieve accurate predictions is a clever way to learn how the model interprets patterns in structured data. Looking forward to experimenting with it! 👍🚀

entry.title: Re: Trial Cloud Identity Services: first administrator ne...
entry.content: I received the email to set up my admin account, but now I get redirected to /admin/forbidden.html with error message "You entered a valid URL, but you are not authorized to view the content; contact your system administrator".

entry.title: Навчальні пропозиції SAP – Червень 2026 | Розклад вебінарів
entry.content:
Шановні учасники спільноти SAP Community Kyiv,

Раді поділитися насиченим розкладом навчальних сесій SAP на червень 2026 року!☀️ Обирайте теми, які вас надихають, реєструйтесь заздалегідь та додавайте до календаря — червень пройде з користю! 

Час вказано за Києвом | Мова вебінарів — англійська.

Хмарна трансформація та міграція

  • 16 червня, 15:00 – 16:00
    Accelerate Cloud Migrations with Migration Assistants and SAP Cloud ALM
    Дізнайтеся, як SAP переосмислює хмарну трансформацію, розвиваючи SAP Cloud ALM до рівня Платформи виконання трансформацій. Завдяки вдосконаленому інструментарію на основі агентів клієнти можуть прискорити налаштування, підготовку даних, міграцію та автоматизоване тестування, використовуючи потужність Агентного ШІ.

AI та дані

  • 9 червня, 18:00 – 19:00
    SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps – Data & AI Content Updates
    Приєднуйтесь до живої сесії, щоб дізнатися про останні оновлення SAP Enterprise Support, зокрема:
    • SAP Datasphere у Business Data Cloud: розгортання CQC та оптимізація рішень
    • SAP HANA Cloud: серія майстерень з впровадження за участю експертів
    • SAP Business AI & Joule: серія майстерень з впровадження за участю експертів
  • 11 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    From AI Subscription to AI Consumption: The New Commercial Model After Sapphire
    Після оголошень на Sapphire SAP поступово переходить від традиційної ліцензійної моделі на основі користувачів до гнучкої моделі споживання на основі агентів. Значна частина вбудованих можливостей переходить у категорію Free AI, тоді як нові розширені агенти та асистенти займають їх місце. Цей перехід формує нову комерційну парадигму, орієнтовану на масштабованість, прозорість використання ШІ та тісніший зв’язок між бізнес-цінністю ШІ та операційними моделями підприємств.
  • 25 червня, 16:00 – 17:00
    SAP Runs SAP BDC – Unleashing Human & Artificial Intelligence with Data Products
    Дізнайтеся, як SAP розкриває потенціал людського та штучного інтелекту, використовуючи продукти даних у SAP Business Data Cloud. Ви побачите, як SAP запускає маховик ШІ, даних та застосунків зсередини, та як перетворює SAP BDC на реальні рішення для всієї компанії. На реальних прикладах ви дізнаєтесь, як операціоналізувати продукти даних для прискорення аналітики, розширення можливостей команд та впровадження інновацій на основі ШІ у масштабі.
  • 30 червня, 16:00 – 17:00
    5 Lessons from SAP’s Own Transformation to an Agentic AI Enterprise
    Операційна модель Enterprise AI від SAP забезпечує чіткість та узгодженість у середовищі агентного ШІ. Ви отримаєте уявлення про Golden Path агентного ШІ від SAP — комплексну структуру для побудови, розгортання та управління корпоративними ШІ-агентами на SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) у масштабі.

AI та операції

  • 18 червня, 16:00 – 17:00
    From AI-Driven Insights to Automated Operations with SAP Automation Pilot and SAP Cloud ALM
    Дізнайтеся, як SAP Automation Pilot інтегрується з SAP AI Core для аналізу операційних даних та перетворення аналітики на дії. Сесія демонструє, як різні типи метрик та дані спостережуваності збираються SAP Automation Pilot, аналізуються SAP AI Core та передаються до SAP Cloud ALM для розширеної візуалізації та сповіщень. Цей підхід забезпечує справжню модель операцій із замкненим циклом, де проблеми виявляються, аналізуються та автоматично усуваються.

Архітектура та безпека

  • 10 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    The Trust Triad: A Playbook for Architecting Secure and Sovereign SAP Landscapes
    Архітектура, безпека та суверенітет — це більше не три окремі розмови. Доктор Міхаель Блашке представляє «Тріаду довіри» — практичний посібник, який об’єднує ці дисципліни на етапі проектування, щоб безпека та суверенітет були закладені з самого початку, а не додавалися постфактум.
    Що ви дізнаєтесь:
    • Тріада довіри: конвергенція архітектури, безпеки та суверенітету
    • Модель спільної відповідальності SAP та її вплив на проектування
    • Практичні підходи до інтеграції безпеки та суверенітету в архітектуру
    • Адаптація SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework для суверенних середовищ
    • Реальні приклади з військового та державного секторів
      Для кого: корпоративні архітектори, офіцери безпеки, власники хмарних стратегій та керівники SAP-програм.
      ❓ Останні 10 хвилин — жива сесія Q&A. Готуйте питання!

Комерція

  • 17 червня, 12:00 – 13:00
    Agentic B2B Commerce: Finding the Fast Track to Profitable Growth
    SAP представляє новий сервіс хостингу JavaScript наступного покоління, розроблений для забезпечення роботи високопродуктивних хмарних вітрин у SAP Commerce Cloud. Сервіс надається через SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) та пропонує гнучкість, масштабованість і зручні інструменти для розробників для сучасних headless та composable commerce рішень.
  • 18 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    Decoupled JS Hosting for Commerce Storefronts
    SAP представляє новий сервіс хостингу JavaScript наступного покоління для високопродуктивних хмарних вітрин на SAP Commerce Cloud. Через SAP BTP новий сервіс пропонує гнучкість, масштабованість та зручні інструменти для розробників для сучасних headless та composable commerce рішень.

HR та досвід співробітників

  • 21 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    AI, Data, and Experience: The New Blueprint for HR Service Delivery
    Сучасні співробітники очікують від HR-підтримки безперебійності, персоналізації та миттєвої реакції. Дізнайтеся, як SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management забезпечує перехід від реактивних моделей обслуговування до проактивної підтримки на основі ШІ, що інтегрує самообслуговування, автоматизацію, аналітику звернень та контекстну аналітику. За результатами сесії ви отримаєте чітку структуру для побудови інтелектуальної HR-сервісної моделі, орієнтованої на досвід співробітників.

Навігація та інструменти SAP

  • 23 червня, 16:00 – 17:00
    New in SAP for Me: Your Intelligent Way to Navigate SAP
    Дізнайтеся, як «Інтелектуальні вхідні двері» SAP for Me надають єдину точку входу на основі ШІ для швидкого пошуку відповідей, підтримки та послуг. Платформа поєднує рекомендації ШІ з центром завдань, роблячи взаємодію швидшою, простішою та ефективнішою.

Інтеграція

  • 30 червня, 17:00 – 18:00
    SAP Integration Suite – Monthly Update June
    SAP Integration Suite — це iPaaS платформа для проектування, розробки, виконання та управління інтеграційними потоками, API та бізнес-подіями, що з’єднують хмарні та локальні процеси, застосунки і дані.
    У програмі: Product Updates та інтерактивна сесія з експертом — Integration Black Belt або #AskAnythingIntegration, де панель експертів відповість на ваші запитання.

Якщо побачили щось цікаве — переходьте за посиланнями у назвах вебінарів, реєструйтесь, поки є місця, запрошуйте колег та діліться знаннями. Всі сесії проходять онлайн, більшість буде доступна у записі🎥

entry.title: Re: WorkforcePerson Integration from SF to S4 HANA via MDI
entry.content:
Good day @KKhadhar 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, your current approach is the correct supported one in Public Cloud. The Public Cloud does not offer a standard Position field in the local Workforce object that is automatically read and stored from MDI as a standard field. Workforce extensibility is provided for this case by creating a custom field in Manage Workforce and mapping the incoming MDI field through the BAdI WFD_MDI_EXT_FIELD_MAPPING.

See Extensibility in Manage Workforce App | SAP Help Portal for more insights.

In simple terms:

  • SuccessFactors and MDI may contain Position, but Public Cloud does not provide a standard equivalent workforce field for it.
  • If the field is not part of the delivered standard worker model in S4H Cloud, you cannot consume it as a standard field.
  • The supported solution is for you to create a custom field and map the MDI value with WFD_MDI_FIELD_MAPPING.
  • If your BAdI mapping is already working, then you are following the expected extensibility approach.
  • A standard field-based replication would only be possible if Position was delivered as a standard supported field in S4H Cloud workforce object in a future release.

I recommend the following:

  1. Keep the custom field in Manage Workforce for Position.
  2. Continue using WFD_MDI_EXT_FIELD_MAPPING to map the MDI Position value.
  3. Check the MDI workforce field mapping to confirm whether Postion is available in MDI but not mapped to a standard S4H Cloud workforce field.
  4. If business requires this as a delivered standard field, then consider raising an SAP influence request.

Here are references for more guidance

Field Mapping Between Employee Central and SAP Master Data Integration: Workforce Person | SAP Help Portal

Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence

 

Here is a quick system check guide:

Open Custom fields, check that the Position custom field is created for the Workforce business context.                         

In Custom Logic, check implementation of WFD_MDI_EXT_FIELD_MAPPING, replicate one employee from SuccessFactors through MDI.                 

Then open Manage Workforce and confirm that the custom Position field is correctly populated.

 

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Margin Analysis Allocation in SAP S/4HANA – How to Display Percentage Shares per Receiver?
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I am currently working with the Margin Analysis Allocation in SAP S/4HANA (Fiori) and I am running into a transparency issue I hope someone can help me with.

Scenario: We allocate costs from sender objects (e.g. cost centers) to receiver objects (e.g. profit centers) using actual revenues as the allocation base. The logic works as expected: the higher the revenue of a profit center, the higher its share of the allocated costs. For example, if three profit centers have revenues of 600,000 €, 300,000 €, and 100,000 €, the costs are distributed at 60 %, 30 %, and 10 % respectively.

What works: The Fiori app Allocation Results clearly shows the credits on the senders and the debits on the receivers, including the amounts per sender-receiver relationship and the executed allocation cycle/segments. So the what (which amounts were posted) is fully transparent.

My problem: There seems to be no standard report showing the percentage share each receiver obtained from the allocation. In practice, I would like to answer questions such as:

  • What percentage of the total allocation did a specific profit center receive?
  • On which revenue base (tracing factor) was this share calculated?
  • How can I make the distribution transparent to profit center managers or auditors without reverse-engineering the percentages from the posted amounts?

My questions to the community:

  1. Is there a standard Fiori app or report that displays the percentage shares / tracing factors per receiver after an allocation run?
  2. If not, what is your best practice to close this gap – custom CDS views, analytical queries, or direct reporting on the allocation tables?
  3. Are there any SAP Notes or roadmap items addressing this transparency topic for Margin Analysis Allocation?

Any input, experience, or pointers would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance and best regards!

entry.title: Re: The SAP support Portal does not allow me to select th...
entry.content: Hi, I'm facing the same problem but the strange thing is that I can choose any system at one customer's account and can't choose at onother one. In both accounts I have Super Administrator authorizations and granted authorizations to send and process cases. Did anyone solve this?

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 07 - Authorization annotations
entry.content: Great explanation of CAP authorization concepts! The comparis makes it much easier to understand how role-based access control is implemented in CAP. The challenge is a nice way to reinforce the use of convenience annotations and their underlying authorization definitions. Thanks for sharing this practical security-focused question!

entry.title: Chave de acesso 53 Digitos referente ao novo modelo de NFSe (Padrão Nacional)
entry.content:
Bom dia!!!Estamos sendo questionados pelo negocio, com relação a chave de acesso da NF-e, com 53 dígitos.

 

Sabemos o padrão legal da NF-e no Brasil deve conter exatamente 44 dígitos conforme Manual de Integração da NF-e (Ajuste SINIEF 07/05).

 

Temos alguma mudança nesse sentido?

entry.title: Re: WorkforcePerson Integration from SF to S4 HANA via MDI
entry.content: Interesting requirement. From my experience, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud does not provide a standard Position field equivalent to the SuccessFactors Position object via MDI. Using a custom field with BAdI WFD_MDI_EXT_FIELD_MAPPING is currently a common approach. It would be worth checking the latest SAP documentation and release updates for any new standard mapping support. If a standard field is mandatory, an SAP incident may help clarify the recommended solution and roadmap.

entry.title: Re: SF - Story Reporting - Time Tracking - Display of weekdays
entry.content: hi did you find a solution for this ? if yes could you please share? i am trying to show full working weekdays or either full month for a report but i cant find any source for this

entry.title: Re: Join our Espresso Session: Latest Innovations and Autonomous Asset Management for Utilities
entry.content: Great session for utilities and asset-intensive organizations. The combination of AI, automation, and SAP Intelligent Asset Management highlights how businesses can move from reactive operations to predictive and data-driven decision-making. I'm particularly interested in the predictive maintenance and AI-powered field service capabilities, as they can significantly improve asset reliability, workforce efficiency, and operational performance. Looking forward to learning more about Joule agents and agentic workflows in action. 🚀

entry.title: Re: Managing Multiple Employments in SAP SuccessFactors Learning: Person-Centric Experience
entry.content:
@Romina65 thank you for your question.

I will try to answer in general and if this is not clear please DM me the name of your company and LMS URL where you are thinking to turn on PCX to take further look at your instances.

We are asking customers to use an extra system to not disrupt the day to day activities.

From your message, it sounds that you have the standard trio of instances (production, development, test). If you were to convert test, then you would have to conduct your release testing in the PCX environment - which is different than your production system and this isn't ideal.
Development (or test@prod) can be a candidate to migrate to PCX as well now that the 1H2026 production release has been delivered. So you could use dev instead and keep production and preview aligned.

Finally, regarding a "rollback option", you are correct a system refresh can be used. What does this mean? Say for example you decide to migrate your development tenant to PCX, but after testing it out you realise this doesn't work for you at this time and you prefer to wait and not use PCX. Then you could run a system refresh using your production (non PCX) as the source system for the refresh. Since PCX is not enabled there, it will overwrite the PCX migration on your development instance (the target system of the refresh) thus reverting your Dev instance to it's previous non-PCX state.

entry.title: Re: CDS Table Entity
entry.content:
Hi,
I am working with CDS table entities in a managed RAP business object.

Can I use the transaction SCDO to generate change document objects for these entities, or is there a different standard approach to implement a change history for them?

Thanks and best regards, Christian

Christian

entry.title: Is material type INTR (Intramaterial) still officially supported in S/4HANA 2023/2025?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I've been researching the material type INTR (Intramaterial), which was originally designed for the Process Industry (PP-PI) to represent in-process intermediate materials within a Master Recipe / Process Order.

After extensive research, I have the following findings and open questions:

FINDINGS: -  There is no possibility to create independent Inspection Lots for INTR , unless its manual

 - Quality control for INTR is handled indirectly via Inspection Type 03 (in-process inspection) on the HALB/FERT header material of the Process Order, with MICs assigned at the phase level in the Master Recipe . However, inspection results are recorded against the ORDER/OPERATION, not against the INTR material itself , meaning there is NO direct QM traceability at material level for INTR

- There is no Simplification List item (S4TWL) exists mentioning INTR as deprecated or obsolete in any S/4HANA release

 OPEN QUESTIONS:

1. Is material type INTR still officially supported in S/4HANA 2023 / 2025 On-Premise and Private Cloud?

2. Has SAP ever published any official statement, SAP Note, or roadmap item indicating that INTR is deprecated, to-be-deprecated, or discouraged?

3. Whats the benefit of Intramaterials ? It can be replaced in all cases by HALB.

4. Are there any active S/4HANA customers or industries (pharma, chemical, food & beverage) still using INTR in production systems?

Any guidance from SAP product management, consultants, or experienced practitioners would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Managing Multiple Employments in SAP SuccessFactors Learning: Person-Centric Experience
entry.content:
@sanchobe thank you for your question.

Could you please share more details about the "SAP services" used for user data sync?

Otherwise, if you look at the Person Connector workbook (zip file with all PCX related documents linked in the main blog post) you will see that we require the mapping of the following EC fields: person_id_external (a user facing customisable ID), userid (the employment id), Person_guid (system unique person ID). These fields are already supported in the SF User Connector today, the difference in the Person Connector is how we process the data (person vs employment data) so you should already be able to sync them. In the event where you have issues in the current framework please share (here or DM) more details about the use case and current process.

Note that the Person ID is an EC specific identifier and this is not used in LMS so it isn't mapped to sync via the connector.

entry.title: Re: All fields are not coming in Report Page in Story Report
entry.content:
Hi,

You can try to cross-tab and it will show.. Option 1
You extract the report with all data.. then it will show in excel sheet. Option 2.

I also faced many times this kind of issue.. may be need to check with SAP.

Thanks

entry.content: yes, it was open for the original movement, there has been no change since it. That's why I am surprise

entry.title: Re: How to find version of sap ETD( SAP enterprise threat...
entry.content: We are trying to upgrade ETD reference https://me.sap.com/notes/3313550 but SAP note 1758054 is in upgrade phase.

entry.title: Re: Alternative Standard Mobile Application for Batch-Man...
entry.content:
Good day @mertatay 

Thank you for your question.

The error you described most likely means that the Pick Outbound Delivery app does not support maintaining deliveries with batch split or batch managed items. There is no configuration switch to make Pick Outbound Delivery support batch managed delivery items. This is a standard product limitation of the app. For batch managed outbound delivery processing, use Manage Outbound Deliveries Instead.

If you required barcode scanning on handheld devices without EWM, you would usually need a custom mobile app or side-by-side extension using released APIs.

The recommended standard process is as follows:

  1. Use Manage Outbound Deliveries,                                                                                           
  2. search the outbound delivery, go to item details,
  3. maintain or confirm the batch, enter the picked quantity,
  4. save the delivery and post Goods Issue.

For batch determination, ensure that batch management and batch determination are correctly maintained.

See Batches in Delivery Management | SAP Help Portal for more insights.

Check KBA 3699741 if PGA fails with missing batch.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Custom App Interface Publishing Issues of ABAP Development Tools platform
entry.content:
HI,

In the SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment, developers use the ADT (ABAP Development Tools)platform to create custom application tables and publish them as OData interfaces for externalsystem calls. At the same time, certain fields (such as sapdatakey3 ) need to be controlled as read-only on the frontend Fiori UI. The following issues were encountered during implementation:

  1. When the interface is called, data is only written to the draft table, not the base table.
  2. Attempts to use field (features : instance) in the behavior definition to dynamically controlread/write operations fail because HTTP headers cannot be reliably obtained to determine thecaller source
  3. Using field (features : instance) in the behavior definition to control field read-only with%field-sapdatakey3 = if abap behv=>fc-f-read only results in the interface being unable to writedata.
  4. After disabling draft mode, the "Create" and "Edit" buttons disappear from the frontend Fioriapplication.

entry.title: Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP certified - Positioning the Autonomous Enterprise
entry.content:
Customers are excited about AI. They’re also asking tougher questions.

How do autonomous business processes work? What makes enterprise AI different? Where does Joule fit into the bigger picture?

The thing is, having great technology is one part of the story. Being able to explain the value in a way that resonates with customers is what creates meaningful conversations.

That’s exactly why we’re hosting the Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP Certified – Positioning the Autonomous Enterprise.

In 90 minutes, we’ll walk through the Autonomous Enterprise strategy, the five Autonomous Domains, SAP Business AI Platform pillars, and how Joule assistants and agents help organizations scale intelligence across business processes.

Whether you're preparing for certification or looking to strengthen your customer-facing messaging, this session will help you connect strategy, technology, and business outcomes with greater confidence.

Register now and join us. https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/jun/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014374.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #BusinessTransformation #EnterpriseAI #SAPLearning #PartnerSuccess

entry.title: Re: Using BVB_LIST for pricing Condition Contract
entry.content: Becouse also I would like to use for pricing not just BV... that is the topic. I need to move the ref contract determine by line to KOMP.

entry.title: Re: Trial Cloud Identity Services: first administrator ne...
entry.content: Same issue ... i get redirected to forbidden.html ...

entry.title: BO 4.3 SP05 upgrade failing because of SQL Anywhere logs
entry.content:
Hello guys
I'm having big problems upgrading an SAP Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 system from SP04 patch 8 hotfix 1 to SP05 patch 5.
I have already upgraded the quality system of the same landscape, but I can't do it in production.
It’s installed on a Virtual Machine running Windows Server 2022, using the SQL Anywhere database that comes bundled.
The system is stand-alone, a single node, and the database is on the same VM on which the SIA and Tomcat also run.
The version of SQL Anywhere is 17.11.7458.
In SQL Anywhere, there are only two databases, the CMS database and the audit database.

So far, I have tried THREE TIMES to upgrade it, but, it has always ended with error, and the behavior has always been the same:
after the deployment of the webapps, the upgrade procedure tries to start the SIA but fails.
In the "setupengine.log" file I see 119 attempts to start the SIA that are unsuccessful, for about 20 minutes, after which the procedure continues but, in the end, reports “ends with errors”.
At the same time as these attempts to start to the SIA, there are numerous error entries in the Windows event viewer. Approximately every 15 seconds:


SQLANYs_SQLAnywhereForBI: Cannot open transaction log file -- Can't use log file "<PATH>\BI4_Audit.log" since it has been used more recently than the database file
SQLANYs_SQLAnywhereForBI: Cannot open transaction log file -- Can't use log file "<PATH>\BI4_CMS.log" since it has been used more recently than the database file

At the end of the activity, the SIA does not even starts.
Regarding the preparation of the activity, I can tell you that I have carried out all the steps reported in the two SAP notes 1952120 and 1757132.
After the first attempt, I realized that, indeed, the two transactional log files were quite large. So, I repeated a second time after making an image backup with truncate logs, but the upgrade went exactly the same as the first time.
For the third attempt I tried to install the VC++ redist 2013 libraries since there was only v14 on the machine, but even this didn't change anything.
So, the upgrade procedure performs some operation that corrupts the database by ending the activity with errors and leaving the system unusable.
What kind of checks can I do to try to figure out what's wrong before trying again for the fourth time?

Please help me!!! 

Thank you all guys.

Andrea

entry.title: Re: Issue after an ECC to SAP S/4HANA conversion regardin...
entry.content: I was expecting something along those lines, but surprisingly, SAP Support is actually suggesting that we delete the records directly from the standard tables before rebuilding the commitments.

entry.title: Pay Equity Agent
entry.content:
Dear community,

I have noticed that in Action for All plans you can now configure Pay Equity Agent but I did not find any KBA which would explain how it works and what can be done with it.

Is anyone aware of this feature and how to use it? 

Pay Equity is a big topic in EU and it would be great if it could help us with upcoming salary review cycle.

Thank you.

Frank

entry.title: Re: PoC Real in CNE5 w/o values PS
entry.content: Are you using Progress Analysis calculated POC for Results Analysis or just using it for Earned Value calculations?

entry.title: Re: Error message KI235 in MIGO in a canceliation document
entry.content:
So you have to check/compare the settings of the 2 movement types (the original and the reversal movement type)

Perform the following checks:

  • Field Status for Additional Account Assignment

    • Compare the field status of the "Additional Account Assignment" in the customizing transactions: OMBW (for movement types)  OMCJ (for special stock)
    • Ensure the field status of the reversal movement type (e.g., 552) matches that of the original movement type (e.g., 551).

  • Account Assignment Check in OMWN

    • Review the customizing transaction OMWN for the reversal movement type.
    • Verify that the "Check Account Assignment" field (T156X-XPKON) is configured identically to the original movement type (if unset for resersal type, can trigger clearing of assignments)

  • Price Difference Line Handling

    • If a price difference line is generated, use OKB9 to define an automatic account assignment for the price difference account.

entry.title: Re: ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code is now available on the VS Code marketplace!
entry.content:
@sratz @thomasalexander_ritter 
Will be a data preview option for tables/cds views as in eclipse adt ??

entry.title: Mass update of delivery schedule prices after modify info record price
entry.content: Hi, I have a scheduling agreement in the test environment, number 5500000029 with 5 line items. The validity date is consistent. I changed the price of info record 5300004060. However, when running the job for the program 'Determine and Update Price of Contract Item', the price does not update. In the first run, it updated the price of the second line item, but incorrectly entered '2' (while the info record price was 3.14). Why is it not working? Is this a bug or am I missing a step? Or maybe I'm not using the right program. Is there another one that does this?

entry.title: Re: Change WBS Element Status FIORI App is not showing WB...
entry.content: Yes, all required configurations are in place and the access is working when tested with SAP_ALL assignment. However, unfortunately, nothing is getting triggered or captured in the trace or system logs, which is making it difficult to identify the exact authorization check or failure point.

entry.title: Re: The Future of ABAP is Here: VS Code ADT, Zero-Config MCP, and AI Co-pilots
entry.content: @fabi2295 it will be supported on the next releases they say.

entry.title: Re: QC21 without usage decision
entry.content:
In standard SAP Quality Management, QC21 (Quality Certificate for Inspection Lot) is designed to work only after a Usage Decision (UD) has been made. The logic is that the certificate reflects the final accepted/rejected status of the lot, so SAP requires the UD as a prerequisite.
Having said that, no configuration exists to make QC21 work without a Usage Decision. If you need certificates earlier, you must either enter a preliminary UD, build a custom certificate program, or use COA functionality.

entry.title: Supplier Confirmation Dunning is not being sent
entry.content:
Hello,

we are currently facing the problem that the Supplier Confirmation Dunning is not being sent. But all conditions are fulfilled to send it. This happens occasionally.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Introducing Our New Reply Progression Badges and a Color Refresh!
entry.content:
Glad I made it before the polls closed!

As one who sat through a few SAP user accessibility sessions (when we had big face-to-face things), I wanted to add on to my earlier comment on color contrast. I took the liberty of clipping the posted color range chart, and sprinkled a little infographics on top. While I was photo tweaking I remembered "buddy tags" used for swimming pool safety classifications (non-swimmer, beginner, swimmer) that are physical tags with color added. Blue for beginner, add red for swimmer; no color not tested or not allowed in the deep end. Same idea - separate colors and characters that need to be legible.

I blocked off the numbers and set that contrast to the max: white on black for the 30 and 75 levels only.

(something about 1900-ish works by W.E.B duBois I'll find later)

2 of 5 contrast changed.

 

Buddy tags (from a PDF / app); colors added by me.

Buddy tag demo art

The circular band has milestones for distance in Imperial yards and fractional miles, as a bonus data gauge.

entry.title: Re: Supplier Confirmation Dunning is not being sent
entry.content:
Hi,

Even if all configuration conditions are met, the reminder is generated only when the purchase order fulfills all prerequisite conditions at the time the background job runs. This includes confirmation control settings, monitoring period, supplier acknowledgement status, and overall PO eligibility during job execution.

If any of these conditions change between PO creation and job execution (for example, supplier confirmation is received, the PO is changed, or the monitoring period has not yet been reached at runtime), the PO will be skipped for that particular run. This can lead to the perceived intermittent behavior where some purchase orders receive reminders while others do not.

KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0003754711 explains possible reasons. Please refer to the same.

Thanks and regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 03 - Declarative field control with @mandatory
ABAP SAPUI5 1.71.82
entry.content:
Hello

I have applied the note3756746 :  ABAP SAPUI5 1.71.82, to update library SAPUI. Everything was fine, but the version is not updated

/sap/public/bc/ui5_ui5/resources/sap-ui-version.json

"name": "SAPUI5 Distribution", "version": "1.71.81", "buildTimestamp": "202603251409",

The log; Completely implemented

Thanks

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Issue with PO Item level conditions during Third-part...
entry.content:
Why this happens is because, Condition determination is tied to document type + item category at the time of adoption. Changing the PO type after conditions are already determined does not trigger a fresh pricing procedure determination. SAP assumes that if you change the PO type, you will manually re‑determine pricing if needed.

The standard way is to use the Update Conditions function, or you can automate it with a small enhancement in ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST.

entry.title: Re: Integration Center Version Limitation
entry.content:
You can either save the integration as a new copy or download the .icd file and import it as a new version. Make sure to check destination settings once you do this.

This is the only way to work around the version control in IC.

entry.title: SAP PS Progress Tracking for Physical Weightage % and Financial POC
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I need guidance on the best approach to achieve a client requirement in SAP Project System (PS) in SAP S4/HANA Cloud Private Edition for tracking both:

  1. Physical progress based on activity weightage
  2. Financial progress based on planned cost versus actual cost

The business requirement is simple, but I am unsure about the best SAP PS design/configuration approach.

Business Background

The client’s project planning and execution work is mostly outsourced to vendors.

Internally, the business currently maintains an Excel sheet where:

  • A building/project component is tracked as a major scope item
  • Each scope item has multiple activities
  • Each activity has an assigned weightage
  • Progress is entered manually against each activity
  • The weighted progress rolls up to the overall project progress

Now the client wants to move this tracking into SAP PS.

Proposed SAP Structure

I am considering the following structure:

  • Building / major scope item = WBS element
  • Excavation, superstructure, finishing, etc. = Network activities
  • Each activity will have an individual weightage
  • Activity progress should roll up to the WBS/project level

At the end, total project progress should result in 100%.

Scenario 1: Physical Progress Based on Weightage

Example:

Object Weightage
Activity A5% of total project
Confirmation entered30% complete
Expected project-level POC contribution1.5%

Calculation expected:

 

Activity Weightage × Activity Completion %
= 5% × 30%
= 1.5% overall project progress

 

So if an activity has a weightage of 5% in the total project and the user confirms 30% completion for that activity, the project-level physical progress should increase by 1.5%.

Question

What is the best SAP PS measurement method to achieve this?

The requirement is basic: business only wants to track percentage completion based on activity weightage and roll it up to WBS/project level.

Scenario 2: Financial Progress Based on Planned Cost vs Actual Cost

There is another requirement for financial progress.

Example:

 

Total planned cost = 100,000
Actual cost incurred = 30,000
Expected financial POC = 30%

 

In this case, the client wants financial progress to be calculated based on actual cost incurred against planned cost.

Expected calculation:

 

Actual Cost / Planned Cost × 100
= 30,000 / 100,000 × 100
= 30%

 

This financial POC may also be used as a basis for invoice/payment monitoring.

Question

What is the best SAP PS approach for this financial progress scenario?

Should this be achieved through:

  • Progress Analysis using cost-based method?

Main Requirement

I need to support both scenarios:

1. Physical Progress

Based on activity weightage and manually entered activity completion percentage.

Example:

 

Activity weight = 5%
Activity completion = 30%
Overall project POC contribution = 1.5%

 

2. Financial Progress

Based on actual cost incurred versus planned cost.

Example:

 

Planned cost = 100,000
Actual cost = 30,000
Financial POC = 30%

 

“What I have already tried”

  • I created a simple test case with one WBS element and one network activity, maintained planned cost on the activity, assigned a Progress Version, and executed CNE1 in update mode for the same progress version.

  • I assigned the Estimate Method for progress analysis and entered manual POC/confirmation percentage. After this, I can see the percentage being stored in the progress-related table, for example EVPOCNV shows Progress Version 100 with 30% POC, so the manual progress entry seems to be captured.

  • I also checked the cost/value category side: planned cost is visible in RPSCO, value categories are maintained in CJVC, and the relevant cost element was added to the cost element group in KAH3. However, even after these checks, CNE5 still does not show the expected POC / earned value output.

I am looking for the most practical and reliable SAP PS solution.

Any suggestions, configuration pointers, or recommended design approach would be highly appreciated.

Thank you.
Syed D.

entry.title: How to add custom fields to Fiori App Manage Customer Line Items?
entry.content:
Dear Community,

i want to add custom fields to the Fiori App Manage Customer Line Items F0711.

We are on private cloud 2025 initial shipment.

I noticed that there is an oData v2 Service: FAR_CUSTOMER_LINE_ITEMS and that i should use a Fiori Adaption Project.

So my idea is to extend the original oData Service and add my custom fields. Afterwards create a Adaption Project for the App and change the oData Service to the modified version.

But i dont know how to add custom Fields to this oData Service. I know that i can add fields in SEGW but i dont know how to fill them with data.

Any help is greatly appreciated.

Kind regards

Marcel

entry.title: Re: Composable Storefront local setup and OAuth Framework Update JDK21
entry.content:
Hi  @joris_quenee 

We're upgrading to JDK 21 and, following the steps in your post, we've configured our Composable Storefront version in local environment. However, we're having trouble logging into our store with isolated data, as only the email address is being received when logging into the backend, without the "siteUid".

Do you have any idea why this is happening?

Environment: CCv2, CS 221121.9, Hybris 2211, JDK21

Appreciate for your inputs. 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: OMJI changes are not capturing into a TR.
entry.content:
Hi,

Due to LIS issue, we changed the setup in OMJI from LIS to exclusive block.

While saving, the changes are not capturing into TR.

Kindly suggest us with solution how can we move those changes to PRODUCTION.

regards,

Lakshmi

entry.title: Re: OBYC Account Determination for GBB and ZOF – Balance...
entry.content:
Possible reasons why it was set up this way

1) Special Business Requirement
Few clients want to track certain raw material movements (e.g., subcontracting, consignment, or special stock) on a balance sheet account instead of expensing them immediately. This could have been a way to “park” costs until a later process (settlement, transfer, or manual clearing).

2) Interim Clearing Account
The balance sheet account may have been intended as a temporary clearing account, with periodic manual postings to move balances to P&L. If the follow‑up process was abandoned or forgotten, the account would accumulate balances.

3) Misconfiguration / Error
It is also possible someone mistakenly assigned a balance sheet account instead of a P&L account. This happens if the chart of accounts was not well documented or if the person configuring assumed ROH should always hit inventory accounts.

Risks of leaving it as is

  • Continuous accumulation in a balance sheet account that should be expensed distorts your financial statements.
  • Consumption of raw materials should normally reduce inventory and hit P&L.
  • Auditors will likely flag this as incorrect treatment.

Having said that, the balance sheet assignment under GBB/ZOF for ROH was either a deliberate “parking” design or simply a misconfiguration. If there is no clear business/legal reason, you should switch it to a P&L account and reclassify the accumulated balances.

entry.title: Re: HowTo "SAP Analytics Cloud <-> S/4HANA Integration" in 5 STEPS
entry.content: Is a BTP account obligatoiry? Because in my case, when I try to create a connection in Cloud, it doesn't work even though I've followed all the steps.

Nice blog, am I correct to say the activation of the connection between FG and CIS is done is done in the configuration tile

By default this tile shows my S account, guess it uses the IAS admin user to do the setup, so no need to use the S account?

 

thanks

Sander

entry.title: export time absence calendar into a calendar format
entry.content: Is it possible to export the timer absence calendar into a calendar format at all. We have requests for this from our managers but cannot see any options within SuccessFactors for this.

entry.title: export time absence calendar into a calendar format
entry.content: Is it possible to export the timer absence calendar into a calendar format at all. We have requests for this from our managers but cannot see any options within SuccessFactors for this.

entry.title: Re: Is material type INTR (Intramaterial) still officiall...
entry.content:
INTR is still supported in S/4HANA, not deprecated, but its benefits are limited compared to HALB. Most customers today use HALB, while INTR survives mainly in legacy PP‑PI scenarios.

See the below comparison

AspectINTRHALB
PurposeIn‑process intermediate materialSemi‑finished product
Inventory MgmtTypically not inventoriedFully inventoried
QM TraceabilityNo direct inspection lotsFull QM integration
Use CasePP‑PI recipes, pharma/chemicalsGeneral manufacturing

entry.title: CICD Actions for SAP Integration Suite / The Download → Git → Deploy pipeline
entry.content:
The first post in this series introduced CICD Actions for SAP Integration Suite: what it is, why it exists, and what it looks like from the outside. This post goes inside the machine.

The Download → Git → Deploy workflow is the beating heart of the pipeline. It is the first workflow most teams run when they adopt the solution, and it is the one they use every time a developer changes something on DEV and wants to move it forward. Understanding exactly what happens in each step — and why those steps are designed the way they are — is the foundation for understanding everything else in the system.

  • The Problem It Solves
  • The Git Branch Model
  • What You Fill In
  • Inside the Pipeline
    • Authorization and Environment Resolution
    • Download and Commit to Git
    • Partner Directory in Git: Decoded and Readable
    • Clean Slate Before Deployment
    • Upload and Activate
  • What the Pipeline Does Not Do
  • Key Takeaways

The Problem It Solves

In a typical SAP Integration Suite landscape, a developer makes changes on the development tenant. Those changes live in the SAP Integration Suite design time. When it is time to promote them, someone has to manually export the package, import it into the test system, apply the right configuration values, and deploy each artifact — Message Mappings, Script Collections, Value Mappings, and iFlows. If anything was already deployed in test, it might need to be cleaned up first. Parameters might differ between systems. Log levels need attention. And none of it is recorded anywhere in a way that supports auditability or rollback.

The Download → Git → Deploy workflow replaces all of that with a single button press that is fully automated, fully recorded, and repeatable.

The Git Branch Model

Before walking through the pipeline, it helps to understand how Git is structured in this solution. There are two permanent branches with distinct roles.

The development branch is where all active work lives. Every package download, every configuration change, every new artifact lands here first. This is the branch the Download → Git → Deploy workflow writes to, and it is the branch the deployment jobs read from when uploading to TST.

There is no option to select a different target branch when triggering the workflow. The development branch is hardcoded as the destination. This is a deliberate design choice: developers should not have to think about Git branches at all during the day-to-day download and deploy cycle. The branch model is an infrastructure concern, not a developer concern. As landscape scenarios grow more complex — multiple parallel development streams, for example — this may evolve, but for the common case the simplicity is intentional.

The main branch represents what has been formally released. As long as work is progressing on development, main is untouched. The merge from development into main happens as part of the Release Import process — which creates a Git tag, a GitHub Release, and the clean reference point used for controlled promotion to production. That process is covered in a later post in this series.

What You Fill In

The workflow is (Dev) BTP Download to GIT and Upload/Deploy to TST, triggered manually via workflow_dispatch. The team member fills in three fields:

  • ID — the Integration Package ID or Partner Directory partner ID to process
  • Mode — IntegrationPackages or PartnerDirectory
  • JIRA Story / SNOW Incident — a reference that becomes the Git commit message, linking every change in the repository to a traceable work item

One important note on the ID field: the pipeline always operates on complete entities as its smallest unit. In IntegrationPackages mode, every operation applies to the entire Integration Package — there is no option to download or deploy a single iFlow or other individual artifact. In PartnerDirectory mode, the same principle applies: one complete Partner Directory entry per ID. This applies to all workflows in this CICD solution, not just this one.

All artifacts must be versioned — before triggering the workflow, every artifact in the package must have a saved version in SAP Integration Suite. Draft artifacts are not supported and will cause the download to fail. This is a standard practice in any version-controlled pipeline: only content that has been deliberately saved as a version is considered ready to leave the development tenant.

A note on Package IDs — if the ID in the screenshot looks unusual (KIhovby8X0TS8uXS3Oo1CCAcu), that is intentional. When creating a package in SAP Integration Suite, the ID is a free-text field you set once at creation time. Unlike the package name, it can never be changed afterwards. Using a GUID avoids any confusion down the line: names get renamed, reorganised, or reused — a GUID stays unambiguous forever.

Three fields, one button. The ID and Mode select what to process; the JIRA or SNOW reference becomes the Git commit message, tying every repository change to a traceable work item.

Inside the Pipeline

That is it for the inputs. Everything else is automated. The screenshot below shows a completed run for an integration package — 1m 34s from button press to a fully deployed package on TST. The actual runtime depends on the number of artifacts in the package: the deployment phase is typically the longest part of the process, as all artifacts are activated — Message Mappings, Script Collections, and Value Mappings are always deployed automatically, while iFlows are deployed based on their individual deployment configuration. The sections below explain what each phase does and why it is designed the way it is.

 

Authorization and Environment Resolution

Before anything touches SAP Integration Suite, the workflow verifies that the person who triggered it is a member of the authorized GitHub team. The team slug is defined in the BTP_DEV_TEAM_SLUG repository variable, configured once during the initial repository setup. If the check fails, the run stops immediately — no SAP Integration Suite credentials are used, no API calls are made. This applies to both modes.

Authorization is not just a security gate. It is also where the workflow binds to the DEV GitHub Environment — a named set of secrets and variables scoped to a specific job — and resolves all the connection details — BTP API URL, token endpoint, technical user, and service key identity — that every subsequent step will use. Before the deployment phase, the workflow binds to the TST GitHub Environment in the same way — resolving its own set of credentials and API endpoints. The team membership check is not repeated: it runs once, at the start of the workflow, and covers the entire run. No URLs or credentials are hardcoded anywhere in the workflow. Everything is environment-driven, which means the same workflow definition works across any landscape configuration without modification — adding a stage is a matter of adding a GitHub Environment with the appropriate credentials, not changing the workflow itself.

Download and Commit to Git

Once authorization is confirmed and the DEV environment is resolved, the pipeline checks out the development branch and removes the existing content for the package or Partner Directory ID. This clean-first approach ensures that if an artifact was deleted on DEV, it does not silently survive in the repository. The pipeline then authenticates with SAP Integration Suite, fetches the new content, and commits it — what lands in Git is an exact reflection of what is in SAP Integration Suite at that moment.

For IntegrationPackages mode, the package is fetched as a ZIP archive. The extraction goes two levels deep: the outer package ZIP is unpacked, and each artifact archive inside it is unpacked as well. The result is that real source files — iFlow configurations, Groovy scripts, Message Mappings, Value Mappings, Script Collections — land as individual readable files in Git. Every change to a Groovy script or a mapping is visible in the commit diff and reviewable in a pull request. It also opens the door for automated code scans — static analysis of Groovy scripts for security or quality requirements — as part of custom pipeline enhancements. And because the source files are plain text, the repository's built-in search becomes a lightweight where-used tool: searching across all packages for a specific endpoint URL, a function name, or a configuration value instantly shows every iFlow and script that references it.

For PartnerDirectory mode, the four object types are retrieved individually via API calls and written as four JSON files, one per type: String Parameters, Binary Parameters, Alternative Partners, and Authorized Users.

Regardless of mode, the commit uses the JIRA or SNOW reference entered at dispatch as the commit message. Every change in the repository is therefore traceable to a work item. Configuration and deployment control files for each iFlow are also created or updated as part of the commit — the detail on these is covered in the next post in this series. Crucially, these files are separate from the artifact content that is deleted and re-fetched on each download; any stage-specific parameter values or deployment settings the team has configured are preserved across repeated runs, not overwritten.

Partner Directory in Git: Decoded and Readable

The way Binary Parameters are stored in Git deserves a special mention. In the Partner Directory of SAP Integration Suite, Binary Parameter values are base64-encoded blobs — not human-readable in the portal. The pipeline stores them in two parts. The index file BinaryParameters.json lists each entry with its metadata and a filename reference instead of the raw encoded value. The actual content is decoded and written as a separate file under a Binary/ subfolder, named after the entry.

 

The Value field holds a filename reference, not a base64 blob. The decoded content lives as a separate file in the Binary/ subfolder.

The result is that an XSD schema used for Receiver Determination, or any other binary payload, is stored as its actual readable content in the repository. The example below is a simplified illustration — in practice this file would contain a full XSD definition.

 

The decoded file as it appears in Git. Every change to the content is visible in the commit diff and reviewable in a pull request — something simply not possible with an opaque encoded string.

Clean Slate Before Deployment

Before uploading the new version to TST, the pipeline clears what is already there. This happens in two layers, and the order matters.

For IntegrationPackages mode, the runtime artifacts are undeployed first. The order matters: if the design-time package were deleted while artifacts are still active in the runtime, a runtime restart would leave those artifacts unable to find their definitions — or worse, leave orphaned objects in the runtime that no longer have any design-time counterpart and that nobody remembers deploying. Cleaning the runtime first avoids that risk. Only once the runtime is clear is the design-time package deleted.

For PartnerDirectory mode, there is no runtime deployment to stop. The existing Partner Directory entries are deleted directly from the design time.

In both cases, clearing the design time completely before uploading is what makes the clean-slate guarantee possible. If an artifact was removed from the package on DEV, it will not survive on TST — there is no mechanism by which a deleted artifact can linger in the target environment. Without this step, TST would gradually accumulate ghost artifacts that no longer exist on DEV, making it an unreliable mirror of the source.

Upload and Activate

With the target tenant clean, the pipeline uploads the new content from the development Git branch and activates it.

For IntegrationPackages mode, the package source is reassembled into a ZIP — the format the SAP Integration Suite public API expects for package upload — and uploaded to the TST design time. The deployment engine then activates the artifacts in the package: Message Mappings, Script Collections, and Value Mappings are always deployed automatically. Integration Flows are deployed selectively — each iFlow has a deployment flag in its configuration file committed to Git, and only those marked for deployment in the target environment are activated. Since the package was deleted and re-uploaded, every deployed iFlow is new to the runtime and starts with the default log level of INFO. After deployment, the pipeline updates each iFlow's log level to whatever is configured for that environment in Git. No one needs to visit the operations cockpit to check or adjust anything afterward.

For PartnerDirectory mode, the pipeline creates the Partner Directory entries on the TST Cloud Integration runtime. Support for Edge Integration Cell runtimes is planned and will be added once the required public APIs become available.

What the Pipeline Does Not Do

The Download → Git → Deploy workflow intentionally stops at TST. It does not automatically promote to production. Moving to PRD — or to any further stage — is a deliberate, separate action. The Release Import workflow handles that step, using Git tags and branch comparisons to calculate exactly what has changed since the last release and deploy only the delta. That is the subject of a later post in this series.

Key Takeaways

  • The pipeline operates on complete entities as its smallest unit: one full Integration Package in IntegrationPackages mode, one complete Partner Directory entry in PartnerDirectory mode. Individual iFlows or artifacts cannot be targeted in isolation — this applies to all workflows in the solution.
  • A single workflow dispatch downloads a package from DEV, commits it to Git with a traceable reference, and deploys it to TST — fully automated, no manual handover.
  • The download creates and maintains one configuration file per package containing the parameter values for all iFlows in that package, and one deployment control file per iFlow — all committed alongside the package source in Git. Stage-specific values set by the team are preserved across repeated downloads. The detail is covered in the next post.
  • The clean-before-upload pattern ensures the target environment reflects Git exactly, including deletions.
  • Partner Directory is fully supported by the same pipeline. Switching the mode input is all it takes — the same authorization, Git commit, and deployment steps apply, with Partner Directory-specific handling where the content differs.
  • Every action that happens — every download, every parameter value, every deployment decision — is recorded in Git history.

The next post in this series covers the configuration layer in depth: how externalized parameters and log levels are managed per environment, how to add a new stage, and how to update a parameter value without triggering a full download.

Repository and documentation: https://github.com/SAP/cicd-actions-for-sap-integration-suite

entry.title: Re: we are unable to delete records, no issues and no aut...
entry.content:
To diagnose the root cause of failed deletions in SLT, perform the following checks:

  • Review SLT Replication Logs
    Analyze the SLT replication logs (transaction LTRC) for any errors, warnings, or inconsistencies related to deletion operations.
  • Verify Delete Trigger Configuration
    Confirm the delete trigger settings for the affected table in SLT (transaction LTRS).
    Ensure that the delete trigger is correctly configured and active for the table in question.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to our SAP Learning Group moderated by SAP Learning Experts
entry.content:
Hi  @HAKIM81 Juls-29_

Thanks for your post!
Would you mind sharing your question in English?
This will help us understand your request more clearly and allow everyone to follow up and support you.
Kind regatrds
Margit

entry.title: Creating Customized Internal Order Report, which provides all the details about the IOs created.
entry.content:
Hi ,

We require your support and assistance to fix an ABAP runtime error encountered in the T code  ZFI_BUDGETIO_REPORT in the SAP ECC system.

Background:

  • The IO Report was recently customized and transported to Production last week.
  • Based on user requirements, additional tables were incorporated into the report logic.

Tables currently used:

  • Existing: COEP, COSP, AUFK, COBK, BKPF, BSEG, EBKN, EBAN, BSET

The report was enhanced to fetch and display all required data; however, users are now facing a runtime error while executing the report at foreground, but when we execute are background, it is taking 30K seconds (8hrs) to generate report for 1 month data. 

Thanks,

Lohith

entry.title: Re: Mass update of delivery schedule prices after modify...
entry.content:
Perform the below steps to narrow down the issue

  • Run a test with a new scheduling agreement linked to the same info record. If the price updates correctly, the issue is with the existing agreement’s condition history.
  • If the wrong value (“2”) keeps appearing, check the condition record.  There may be multiple entries, and the system is picking the first valid one.
  • If you want the net price field to always equal the info record price, you may need to adjust the condition technique or use the Mass Change app instead of the update job.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to our SAP Learning Group moderated by SAP Learning Experts
entry.content:
Hi  @riteshwathore 

Here are some recommendations
How to enter the SAP industry as a fresher

  • Access our Learn by industry site
  • Choose a focus area early (e.g., BASIS, HCM, FICO, ABAP)
  • Start with SAP Learning Journeys , they are free on learning.sap.com, you can find a list here
  • Consider foundational certifications to build credibility
  • Build a basic understanding of business processes (very important in SAP)
  • Apply for:
    • Internship roles
    • Junior consultant positions
    • Support roles (best entry point)

Skills required for support consultant roles

  • Understanding of SAP system navigation
  • Basic knowledge of:
    • SAP modules (e.g., HCM, FI, MM)
    • System concepts (especially for BASIS or technical roles)
  • Familiarity with ticketing tools (ServiceNow, JIRA)
  • Ability to analyze issues and identify root causes
  • Basic debugging mindset
  • Clear communication with users
  • Ability to handle incidents and prioritize tasks
  • Customer-oriented approach

 Best ways to gain practical knowledge and job opportunities

  • Use SAP Learning Hub (hands-on systems) Practice Systems Practice your skills in live SAP environments. 
  • Follow guided exercises in learning journeys 
  • Practice through:
    • Trial systems
    • Simulations
  • Join SAP Community / forums
  • Work on real-life scenarios (even self-created ones)

KInd regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 07 - Authorization annotations
entry.content: b6f27f30c72eb20640a6b2d6ae578f5b092c7e9cdf4cdf6cc0a76743004eca17

entry.title: Finally able to join this group!
entry.content:
Thanks @Vaibhav-Mishra for pointing out this group to me! I am an SAP consultant based here in Columbus and open to network with like minded individuals.

My experience has recently been mostly in the Fashion/Retail industries working in OTC/SD related processes. Lately I am working with a major sporting goods manufacturer and a focus on aATP (Advanced Available to Promise) functions like ATP, SUP, PAL, ABC, etc. Ask me questions if you have them and feel free to drop me an invite on LinkedIn.

Cheers,

Justin

entry.title: Set audit trail value for an input field in SAC Planning story
entry.content:
Hello

I am using Rapid FP&A Workforce Planning business content, although I believe that this question applies to any planning situation. 


For input fields in a Story, the audit_trail value is set to "#."  I would like to set it to a specific value since there is a constraint in a data action.  How can I set the audit_trail value--would like to hardcode it rather than having the end user set it. 

Thanks

entry.title: Material Type DIEN in public cloud
entry.content:
Hi Expert, 

I am configured for material type now, but I can't find material type "DIEN" in system. 
Do I lack of any configure/scope, so I cannot find it? Or material type DIEN has already alternated by material type SERV?

 

Tina

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 07 - Authorization annotations
Re: How to Debug SAP CPI Groovy Scripts Locally Using Visual Studio Code.
entry.content:
Hi @JohanCalderon 
 
I followed your steps in the section 'Using SAP CPI Tools Extension,'  but I keep getting the error: 'Connect timed out waiting for SAP CPI Tools Extension in VS Code.' Do you have any idea how to fix it?

I tried using Groovy version groovy-2.4.21 or 2.5.23, also using VS.Code: 1.107.1 or VS.Code: 1.123, and JDK Eclipse Adoptium, FULL_VERSION="1.8.0_492-b09", JVM_VERSION="25.492-b09", using SAP CPI Groovy Script extension version 1.2.4.
 
Please any help welcome, checking the View->Output->SAP CPI Groovy Script is not displayed nothing, it looks like is not started any listener as you mentioned in the blog, also it was set not used Local Proxy in the settings, but anything allowed to run the option SAP CPI Groovy extension well. 

 

Look forward to hearing from you.
Thanks in advance.
Best Regards,
Maria Orellana

Re: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content: Herbert is correct.

entry.title: Budget from ppm to ps
entry.content:
Hi ,

My clients has this requirement wherein they initially allocate minimal budget in ppm ,later if they need additional budget they supplement it in ps. But at some point the ps budget will be more than ppm budget as budget will not sync back to ppm .what is the Standard practice of doing supplements when we have ppm in place. Also they want to have workflows for supplements transfer and returns. 

Can someone please suggest on this.

Thanks &regards,

Manogna

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types
Re: S/4 Hana, Sales Order creation, Downpayment VAT condi...
entry.content: Hello, The issue is solved, I removed the condition group flag in ZTX2 condition type and the value now is correct, thanks anyway for your suggestions

entry.title: Re: Your S/4HANA & Ariba implementation is live, but your GRC ruleset do not have Ariba rules co
entry.content:
Appreciate you taking the time to prepare this.

It's a very interesting take on the problem. 

I'd like to get your view on 2 things:

  • How do you see the effort to create these custom tcodes in DEV and transport to Production in an organization with tight change management controls?
  • How do we keep it in sync with Ariba?

Thanks,

Hi support,

We are not able to see the Connections menu in the left hand side menu bar even after logging in as an administrator. 

attachaed are two images 

  1. HPE-image - Is our datasphere screen showing "Connections" menu is not visible
  2. other-customer-image - is screenshot from other customer's datasphere where connections menu is visible

Vaibhav

entry.title: selection in practice mode does not work the first time
entry.content:
dear support

We are making a recording in the browser on a finance S/4 transaction.

Everything is working well apart from 1 thing:  when clicking in the practice mode on my area, it does not work but when I ask for help it shows me the to be selected field (which is exactly where I Clicked before) but when I click then again, it does work.

Please look at the DKP of the recording step 23 and 30 and  where you need to click on ZACR for example (the blue selected one is not the correct space nut the orange frame is the right one).

It happens on more places but these are some examples.

many thanks in advance for some advice

eric

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Depreciation keys for the assets
entry.content:
Can you create these following Depreciation Keys for the assets:

•    Depreciation key of 5.50% (with a 50% reduction for the first year only)
•    Depreciation key of 1% (with a 50% reduction for the first year only)
•    Depreciation key of 5.56%
•    Depreciation key of 20%

entry.title: Re: Pay Equity Agent
entry.content: https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/use-sap-successfactors-compensation-to-provide-pay-equity-adjustments/bc-p/14400186/highlight/true#M10120

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay: how to correctly configure field format...
entry.content: Delete workseets, change your format to #,##0 then run bonus calculation and relaunch worksheets. Also make sure your business goal achievements are entered at 0.8 for 80% and so on.

entry.title: Re: The Challenge: Large Platform, Demanding Operations
entry.content: @oliweng very interesting blog. Does it work along with CTMS Process?

entry.title: Re: Where is the slide deck for the recorded trainings?
entry.content:
Hi @wsunarko 
At the moment, the functionality on learning.sap.com means that the slides are only available to learners who registered for the session.  Recordings are available to all learners who have a valid subscription.
If you registered for the session, you will find the slides in the My Learning area on against your Past sessions.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: BO 4.3 SP05 upgrade failing because of SQL Anywhere logs
entry.content:
Hi Andrea - That i a rare error  -
It does seem like the sqlA db is corrupted - but not sure if installer did it as we have many other environments where this works perfectly fine. We see this error discussed in kba 2494203 where it suggests the log file was corrupt.
Can you please try the approach as per this kba and see if that helps get sqlA running.
